title: Audit clone-create sites across all agents and add --depth 50 where missing. Specifically locate analyst-baby's clone-create site (operator-direct survey couldn't find it; baby's run summary says it had a 659MB full clone to clean up).
scope_authority: Survey + recommend. Tinker authors PROP-NNN listing all clone-create sites with the depth-flag patch for each. Operator + cowork-claude apply.

bug_summary.observed_event: analyst-baby run summary 2026-05-18T05:14Z reported: 'DISK NOTE: Root partition at 93% full (722MB free). Clone was 659MB. Cleanup will reclaim ~659MB.' This is a FULL clone, not a depth-limited one. Operator was already managing root-partition disk pressure (hit 100% ENOSPC earlier today).
bug_summary.cannot_find: analyst-baby.md inherits Step 0a from analyst.md, which only refreshes (git fetch + pull --rebase) and does NOT create the clone. Operator-direct grep across prompts/ + reference/ did not surface a clone-create command that baby uses. Yet baby clearly HAS a clone (659MB evidence). Possibilities: (a) some pre-flight wrapper outside the prompt creates the clone, (b) baby implicitly clones via gh CLI or similar, (c) the clone is left over from a prior agent run that did not clean up due to uncommitted changes (baby skips cleanup if dirty), (d) a node script in analyst.md or analyst-baby.md does a clone implicitly when reading from CLEAN_CLONE path.

operator_preferences_for_proposal: Surgical: small patch per site (just add --depth 50 to git clone command).
Capability-preserving: --depth 50 covers ~2-3 days of git history at current cadence, plenty for any pull-rebase or git-log query the agents do.
Find ALL sites: if baby is using an uncommon clone source, document it.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Grep all of monitor/prompts/ + monitor/prompts/reference/ + monitor/scripts/ + build-scripts/ for 'git clone' and document every site. Cross-reference against which agents use which sites. Identify sites that already have --depth 50 vs those that don't.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: audit_results section in PROP listing all clone-create sites with current depth setting.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Figure out where analyst-baby actually obtains its clone. Read analyst-baby.md + analyst.md carefully. If the clone source is genuinely inherited from a missing-but-implicit step, document the implicit behavior. If there's a non-obvious clone command, add --depth 50 there.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: baby_clone_source section explaining the root cause.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Author a comprehensive patch set: each clone-create site gets --depth 50 (unless --depth already specified). Include the 4 already-patched sites by operator-direct this morning as already-done in the PROP for completeness.

Counter-arguments: (a) why --depth 50 not --depth 10 (smaller) or --depth 200 (safer for git history queries)? (b) any agent that genuinely needs deep history (e.g., curmudgeon-side drift-audit walks git log)? Be opinionated.

in_scope_for_tinker: Standardize --depth 50 across the estate (or argue for a different value with justification)
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT self-apply any prompt edit
Do NOT redesign the clone discipline architecture — just add the depth flag
related_props: PROP-016 (Mech A NEVER_PUSH detect) — sibling clone-discipline concern
CLAUDE.md 'Per-session clone discipline' (2026-05-09) — operator-side discipline section
deadline_target: Next tinker scheduled run (daily 03:30Z tomorrow). Low priority but disk pressure has bitten 2x today.
